Empires & Puzzles Rhys Review: The Fast Mana Machine & Tile Buffer

Is Rhys still the golden boy of yellow stacks? We dive into his unique tile-altering mechanics, his massive attack buff potential, and whether his 2022 stats can survive the 2026 meta. Plus, why he might be the best friend your Xnolphod never knew he needed.
Rhys: The Fast-Paced Flutist Who Turns Tiles Into Turbo-Chargers
Rhys: The Bard of Valor Who Plays a Fast Game

. Originally released in mid-2022, he entered the game as a high-speed support engine designed to make your team move faster and hit harder. He is a "Jack of all trades" in the best possible way: he heals (boosted health!), he cleanses, he buffs attack, and—most uniquely—he turbo-charges your yellow tiles.

While he lacks a costume to date, his base kit was so forward-thinking that he remains a topic of discussion long after his release. He’s the kind of hero who doesn't just do one thing well; he facilitates everything else your team wants to do.

Rhys sports a distinct "elven bard" aesthetic that leans heavily into the fantasy trope of the agile, charismatic musician. He’s depicted playing a flute, looking rather nonchalant about the chaos of battle. The color palette is a mix of vibrant yellows and deep blues, signaling his Holy alignment and perhaps a touch of royal flair.

Unlike the grimdark warriors or monstrous beasts often found in Challenge Festivals, Rhys looks like he stumbled out of a tavern where he just won a high-stakes card game. He’s not here to smash you with a hammer; he’s here to play a tune that makes his friends invincible while you struggle to find your mana.

In the current meta, Rhys is squishy. Extremely squishy. Without Limit Breaks (LB1 and LB2) and full emblems, he is at risk of being one-shot by modern snipers or wiped out by a single heavy AoE. However, his
is surprisingly high for a support hero of his era, which means his tile damage is respectable—a crucial trait for a hero whose entire gimmick revolves around yellow tiles.

When Rhys was released, he was immediately recognized as a top-tier offensive support. He was the "Yellow Xnolphod" in terms of mana manipulation, but with more offensive bite. He replaced heroes like Professor Lidenbrock in many offensive stacks because he was faster and offered that sweet attack buff.

Mechanically, Rhys is still fantastic. A Fast cleanse + overheal + mana boost is a "dream kit."
However,

He is too fragile for defense. On offense, he is usable but you must protect him.
He is excellent for war attacks, specifically against purple tanks where his yellow stack synergy shines.
The combination of high attack stat (for his era), attack buff (up to 70%), and tile mana generation makes him a staple for high scores on Dark Titans.

If you pull Rhys early, you have struck gold. He will carry you through World Maps and Quests with his overheal. Prioritize leveling him immediately as he can serve as your main healer and tank in lower arenas.

Rhys is a luxury tool. Do not put him on defense; he will be farmed. However, keep him in your war roster. Pair him with modern stats-heavy heroes to balance the team. He is absolutely worth 2LB (Alpha Aethers)
you care about Titan scores or if you lack a modern Fast cleanser. If you have newer 5-star healers with 2025 stats, Rhys might be relegated to your 3rd or 4th war team.
Upon release, players debated Rhys vs. Professor Lidenbrock. The consensus shifted to Rhys being the offensive king due to speed, while the Professor reigned on defense.
A surprising amount of forum debate centered on Rhys's gender due to the androgynous art. (Spoiler: The name is traditionally Welsh and masculine, but in 2022 E&P, who knows?)
In Beta, Rhys was considered underwhelming. He received a massive buff right before release (adding the Heal), which instantly propelled him from "meh" to "must-have." One player noted,
"Hero was buffed: boosts 320 HP & can exceed max HP... at FAST! Rhys is now arguably better than Lidenbrock."

Rhys is a testament to how good game design can outlast power creep. While his numbers are dusty, his mechanics are pristine. He is the lubricant that makes a yellow engine run smoothly. If you can keep him alive, he will reward you with a symphony of yellow tiles and victory screens.
A Titan superstar and offensive staple who needs a bodyguard (or a lot of Aethers) to survive the modern battlefield.
A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Rhys, the Holy Bard from Challenge Festival II. Analyze his tile-boosting mechanics, attack buffs, and modern viability in the current meta.
